title: Bridge Essay: Hearing voices in the resting brain: A review of intrinsic functional connectivi

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Hearing voices in the resting brain: Areview of intrinsic functional connectivity research on auditory verbal hallucinations.** — Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews (2015). PMID: 25956256. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2015.04.016. Score: 50. Resting state networks (RSNs) are thought to reflect the intrinsic functional connectivity of brain regions. Alterations to RSNs have been proposed to underpin various kinds of psychopathology, including the occurrence of auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH). This review outlines the main hypotheses linking AVH and the resting state, and assesses the evidence for alterations to intrinsic connectivity provided by studies of resting fMRI in AVH.
